sprint: Companion Engine Foundation (Sprint C1 -- Lumo v1)
task: Implements the first production-ready Companion Engine using Lumo, the official Story Dragon of VihuPlanet -- the canonical implementation every future companion (Nimbus, Moss, Nova, ...) reuses without any runtime code change. Establishes the Companion Package Contract (assets/companions/lumo/companion.json -- id/name/species/version/defaultState/states -- plus one PNG per declared state), a generic js/companionEngine.js (window.CompanionEngine: load/show/hide/setState/getState/speak/destroy) that knows the Companion Package Contract and nothing else -- zero 'if (id==="lumo")' anywhere in real code, verified via a comment-stripped static scan -- and a Studio-specific js/companionDirector.js (window.CompanionDirector) that is the one file allowed to know Studio's own moments, with companionId treated as a plain configurable default ('lumo'), never a hardcoded branch (also statically verified: exactly one quoted 'lumo' literal in the whole file). Wires the exact choreography specified: Studio opens -> wave + "Let's imagine!" -> idle after 3s (js/app.js boot hook); user starts creating -> think + "I can't wait to see your story!" (js/creationFlow.js's _finish()); user types -> curious, via a document-level delegated capture-phase input listener with a 4s cooldown; user inserts artwork -> celebrate + "That's wonderful!" -> idle after 2s (js/contextPanel.js's _applyImageResult); story published -> celebrate + "Your story is ready!" -> idle after 2s (js/publishStudio.js's _finalizePublish); no interaction for 2 minutes -> sleep; user interacts again -> wave -> idle -- all via a global activity listener's own idle timer. Every hook site is a single, defensive try/catch-guarded line, matching this codebase's established 'thin hook into a dedicated module' pattern (PageRuntime/ObjectStrip/ContextPanel/MagicCard) -- a missing companion.json, a broken image, or a script failing to load leaves Studio's boot sequence completely unaffected. Lumo's 8 PNGs (hero/idle/wave/think/talk/celebrate/sleep/curious, 480x480 transparent) are disclosed, placeholder production art -- a simple Canvas-drawn chibi Story Dragon, since the real production assets folder did not exist in this environment -- built so swapping in final hand-painted art later needs zero engine/integration code changes. The widget renders bottom-right (repositioned after a screenshot review found the original bottom-left placement collided with Screen 1's own decorative Story Meadow signpost). Genuine multi-companion genericity proven directly, not just asserted: a hand-authored second package ('nimbus', with a 'happy' state Lumo doesn't even have, never named anywhere in companionEngine.js/companionDirector.js) loads and runs correctly through the identical, unmodified CompanionEngine. Verified via two new Playwright suites (43 assertions total: package contract validation, static zero-hardcoded-reference checks, the full live boot/think/curious/artwork/publish choreography driven through real UI actions, sleep-after-2-minutes and wake-to-wave-then-idle via Playwright's fake clock, and the nimbus genericity proof) plus the full existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright regression suite (10 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites -- all passing unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; confined to two new modules plus additive, single-line hooks in four existing files.
